CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-18-2026 Continued To Jun-23-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Volkswagen Group Of America, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Jan-29-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'S Motion To Tax Costs.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The motion to tax costs is granted in part. The court strikes $3445 from the cost bill in expert witness fees and otherwise denies the motion to tax costs. The total amount of costs awarded is $6,665.50.

Ordinarily, expert witness fees are not recoverable as costs unless ordered by the court. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 1033.5, subd. (a)(8).) Where a party serves an offer to compromise pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, section 998, this code provides in subdivision (d) as follows: "If an offer made by a plaintiff is not accepted and the defendant fails to obtain a more favorable judgment or award in any action or proceeding other than an eminent domain action, the court or arbitrator, in its discretion, may require the defendant to pay a reasonable sum to cover postoffer costs of the services of expert witnesses . . . ."

Here, Plaintiff has not attached the 998 offer and no judgment has entered. Rather, the notice of settlement anticipates a dismissal by plaintiff, which is treated as a favorable judgment for defendant unless the parties agree otherwise. (The court assumes that the parties' settlement provided that Plaintiff could claim costs; Defendant makes no argument that costs cannot be awarded at all.)

The court denies the motion as to the deposition fees sought by Plaintiff. The costs are recoverable on their face and Defendant has not demonstrated them to be unnecessary.

A proposed order has been submitted by the prevailing party.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-09-2024 Set For Trial On Nov-24-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-06-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF LUIS MORELL'S, AN INDIVIDUAL, Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents; Request For Sanctions.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

Danja Stocca, Esq., of Quill & Arrow LLP, (310) 933-4271, for Plaintiff.

Not argued/contested. Counsel at the outset requests a continuance of the subject motion in lieu of adopting the court's tentative ruling. The matter is discussed. At the concluion of discussion, counsel's request is denied and the tentative ruling is adopted. This motion was filed prior to the date noticed for the deposition and therefore appears to have been premature. CCP sec. 2025.450(a). Moreover, the deposition has been rescheduled by agreement for a date which has not yet arrived. That agreement has been confirmed by Plaintiff's Amended Notice of Deposition. Under these circumstances, the Court finds that this motion is moot and is hereby ordered off calendar. Sanctions are denied in the interest of justice.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (525/JPT)
